LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JUVENTINO ESTRADA'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY'S PERSON(S) MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,250. GRANTED. THE DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT ORGANIZATION IS PROPERLY NOTICED IN SAN FRANCISCO PURSUANT TO CCP 2025.250(B) ("DEPOSITION OF AN ORGANIZATION THAT IS A PARTY TO THE ACTION SHALL BE TAKEN AT A PLACE THAT IS, AT THE OPTION OF THE PARTY GIVING NOTICE OF THE DEPOSITION, EITHER'OR WITHIN THE COUNTY WHERE THE ACTION IS PENDING'") NATIONAL UNION SHALL PRODUCE: (1) FOR DEPOSITION IN SAN FRANCISCO THOSE OF ITS OFFICERS, DIRECTORS, MANAGING AGENTS, EMPLOYEES, OR AGENTS WHO ARE MOST QUALIFIED TO TESTIFY ON ITS BEHALF AS TO THE MATTERS DESIGNATED IN PLAINTIFF'S "NOTICE OF DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF PITTSBURGH, PA'S PERSON(S) MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE RE: DELIVERY OF POLICY" DATED APRIL 22, 2013; AND (2) THE DOCUMENTS DESIGNATED IN EXHIBIT A TO PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE. THE PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER ON THE DATE OF DEPOSITION AND PRODUCTION THAT SHALL OCCUR NO LATER THAN AUGUST 2, 2013, UNLESS THE PARTIES MUTUALLY AGREE. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Specially Prepared Interrogatories; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JOSHUA HAFFNER, ESQ., AND DREW FERANDINI, ESQ., 213.217.5018, FOR PLAINTIFF JUVENTINO ESTRADA.
DOUGLAS IRVINE, ESQ., 213.680.5140, FOR DEFENDANT NATIONAL UNION INSURANCE CO.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification: DEFENDANT NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF PITTSBURGH, PA, A PENNSYLVANIA COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Specially Prepared Interrogatories; Request For Monetary Sanctions is Granted as to Plaintiff's responses to Interrogatory Nos. 3, 9, 15, 18, 21, 24, 27, 35, 38, 41, 44, and 47 (seeking identification of documents) and 1, 5, 6, 7, 16, 33, 36, 39, 42, 45, and 46 (seeking facts). Within 15 days of entry of order, Plaintiff shall serve verified further responses. Denied as to Interrogatory Nos. 2, 8, 14, 17, 20, 23, 26, 34, 37, 40, and 43 (seeking identification of witnesses) and 10, 11, 13, 19, 22, 25, 28 and 29 (seeking facts). Monetary sanctions are denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Notice Of Motion For Order To Compel Deposition Of Deft National Union Fire Insurance Company'S Person(S) Most Knowledgeable; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,250
Off Calendar Jul-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSHUA H. HAFFNER OF KABATECH BROWN KELLNER, LLP, TEL. NO. (213) 217-5018 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. DOUGLAS R. IRVINE OF THE LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH, TEL. NO. (213) 680-5140 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT NATIONAL UNION INSURANCE COMPANY BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JUVENTINO ESTRADA's Motion For Order To Compel Deposition Of Defendant National Union Fire Insurance Company'S Person(S) Most Knowledgeable; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,250.Granted. The deposition of defendant organization is properly noticed in San Francisco pursuant to CCP 2025.250(b) ("deposition of an organization that is a party to the action shall be taken at a place that is, at the option of the party giving notice of the deposition, either'or within the county where the action is pending'") National Union shall produce: (1) for deposition in San Francisco those of its officers, directors, managing agents, employees, or agents who are most qualified to testify on its behalf as to the matters designated in Plaintiff's "Notice of Deposition of Defendant National Union Fire Insurance Company of Pittsburgh, PA's Person(s) Most Knowledgeable re: Delivery of Policy" dated April 22, 2013; and (2) the documents designated in Exhibit A to Plaintiff's Notice. The parties are to meet and confer on the date of deposition and production that shall occur no later than August 2, 2013, unless the parties mutually agree. Monetary sanctions are denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported.
